old_claim.note: Fired Bricks & Early Kilns is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains because Neolithic bread ovens are scoped to early grain-processing and baking practice, while source-backed kiln-fired building bricks are later third-millennium Mesopotamian construction materials.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica's Neolithic overview supports early farming and food-production context but does not identify fired building bricks or brick kilns as a prerequisite for bread ovens.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The bread-oven node's source supports Neolithic food-production context, not a fired-brick construction prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: After fired bricks are source-dated to the third millennium BCE, keeping this edge would make a later building material a predecessor to much earlier bread-oven practice.

ontology_before: Bread Ovens depended on Fired Bricks & Early Kilns, implying kiln-fired building bricks preceded Neolithic bread ovens.
ontology_after: Bread Ovens keeps its agriculture and fermentation context without an anachronistic fired-brick prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: bread_ovens no longer directly depends on fired_bricks_early_kilns.
would_reject_if: The bread_ovens node is later narrowed to baked-brick urban ovens dated after the third millennium BCE.
A source shows the earliest scoped bread ovens required kiln-fired building bricks.
A separate kiln_built_bread_ovens node is added and should carry a fired-brick prerequisite.
short_reason: Fired building bricks are later than the scoped Neolithic bread-oven practice.
